failure_modes.text: Easy fluency can create a false sense of mastery when the learner has not had to retrieve, struggle, or transfer the skill under pressure.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when fluency would otherwise be mistaken for mastery.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test mastery under transfer conditions: easy fluency during practice does not prove the learner can retrieve the skill under pressure or in a new context.

failure_modes.text: Challenge can become counterproductive when support is removed so aggressively that the learner is overwhelmed instead of stretched.
failure_modes.source_quote: Best when support can be calibrated rather than removed entirely.
failure_modes.mitigation: Calibrate challenge to the learner's current edge: removing support so aggressively that they are overwhelmed defeats the purpose of productive struggle.

failure_modes.text: Abstract difficulty can confuse rather than strengthen learning when people never get the concrete building blocks they still need.
failure_modes.mitigation: Provide concrete building blocks before abstract difficulty; confusion from missing foundations is not the same as productive retrieval effort.

premortem_questions.text: What part of this learning path feels smooth only because we have not required retrieval or transfer yet?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when fluency would otherwise be mistaken for mastery.

premortem_questions.text: Where did we remove support so far that the challenge stopped being productive and started becoming overload?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when support can be calibrated rather than removed entirely.

premortem_questions.text: Which concrete building block is still missing beneath the abstraction we are asking people to struggle through?

heuristics.text: Use retrieval, effort, and friction deliberately so understanding is tested for durability rather than assumed from fluency.
heuristics.source_quote: Most useful when fluency would otherwise be mistaken for mastery.

heuristics.text: Calibrate support so the task stretches capability without dropping people into confusion or shutdown.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when support can be calibrated rather than removed entirely.

heuristics.text: Preserve enough concrete structure that the difficulty strengthens transfer instead of becoming empty hardship.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when the challenge is productive precisely because it strengthens transfer.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on productive friction, calibrated support, and anti-false-fluency intervention chunks rather than on generic perseverance rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor motivation-program sprawl because the raw source is already strong on productive challenge and durable mastery.
open_questions: Deep work and resilience are source-real nearby supports, but the intervention layer is cleaner when it stays on difficulty calibration itself.

allies: The raw source has meaningful overlap with resilience, focused practice, and learning-transfer models for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The source is strongest against false fluency, abstraction drift, and under-structured challenge rather than on a broad antagonist list.
structured_tensions: The model has tension with over-smoothing and with abstraction that removes the concrete supports still needed for transfer.
